key_insight: AA-ViT uses anatomical, structural, and frequency guidance in a vision transformer to synthesize contrast-enhanced brain MRI from pre-contrast scans while better preserving tumour and tissue boundaries.
clinical_relevance: If validated further, the approach could reduce reliance on gadolinium-based contrast agents, shorten imaging workflows, and improve access for patients who cannot receive contrast.
method: Input pre-contrast T1, T2, and FLAIR MRI
Anatomically aware vision transformer with structural and frequency guidance
Generates synthetic post-contrast brain MRI for lesion visualization
evidence_signal: On the BraTS 2021 dataset, the model reported higher PSNR and SSIM than prior methods and received a mean clinical rating of 3.94/5 from three neuroradiologists and one neurosurgeon across 19 cases.
watch_item: The clinical evaluation was small and preliminary, so broader multi-site validation is needed before real-world implementation.
paper_title: AA-ViT: Anatomically Aware Vision Transformer with Structural and Frequency Guidance for Contrast Enhanced Brain MRI Synthesis
